senses.definition: to be very near to something or someone in terms of physical distance
to give the impression of having the quality or characteristic described by the following word

examples.x: The sudden announcement of her resignation came as a shock to the entire team.
Winning the championship came as a triumph for the underdog team.
The sudden rainstorm came as a relief during the scorching summer day.
The failure of the project came as a disappointment to the entire team.
His generosity came as a blessing to the struggling family.
Her exceptional talent in music came as a revelation to her friends.
The increase in gas prices came as a burden to many commuters.
Her sudden illness came as a setback to her plans.

senses.definition: to arrive at a particular location or event at the appointed time

examples.x: She decided to come in time for the meeting to make a good impression on her colleagues.
He wanted to come in time for the movie to avoid missing the beginning.
The team was focused on coming in time to finish their project before the deadline.
The student was committed to coming in time for her classes to improve her attendance.
They decided to come in time for the concert to get good seats.

senses.definition: to become visible, especially if something was previously out of sight

examples.x: As we rounded the bend in the trail, a stunning waterfall came into view.
When the mist cleared, the majestic mountain range came into view.
The ship on the horizon gradually came into view as it approached the shore.
The city's skyline came into view as the plane descended for landing.
When the telescope was adjusted, the distant galaxies came into view.

senses.definition: to arrive at a specific location, event, or appointment after the expected or scheduled time

examples.x: I apologize for coming late to the meeting; I got caught in traffic.
She had to come late to the party due to a prior commitment.
It's considered disrespectful to come late to a job interview.
The students who come late to class may miss important information.
Please don't come late to the conference; the keynote speaker will begin on time.

senses.definition: to arrive at a specific location, event, or appointment at the expected or scheduled time

examples.x: I always make an effort to come on time for work to start the day efficiently.
It's essential to come on time for the doctor's appointment to avoid delays.
She consistently comes on time for her fitness classes to get the most out of her workout.
Students are expected to come on time for exams to ensure a fair and organized testing process.
It's polite to come on time for dinner reservations to avoid inconveniencing the restaurant staff.

senses.definition: to arrive at a situation or event equipped with the necessary knowledge, materials, or resources needed for that specific circumstance

examples.x: She always comes prepared for the job interview with a well-organized resume and a list of references.
Students who come prepared for class tend to perform better in their studies.
It's crucial to come prepared for a camping trip with the right gear and supplies.
A responsible traveler will come prepared with travel essentials like passports, visas, and local currency.
To excel in sports, athletes need to come prepared with the right equipment and a well-thought-out training plan.
